headline: Bernard Arnault, generous patron of the arts and lover of tax breaks
description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (5/6). France's richest man is also one of Europe's leading art collectors. Through the Fondation Louis Vuitton, he combines his passion for the arts with highly lucrative tax arrangements.

headline: European Commission fines Google €890 million, risking Trump's ire
In the West Bank, a Palestinian hamlet faces relentless harassment by settlers
Trump threatens Iran with escalation, including strikes on civilian and nuclear infrastructure
description: After two weeks of strikes on Iran that yielded no progress, US President Donald Trump raised the possibility of targeting uranium enrichment sites beneath the Kolang mountain in central Iran.

headline: Fields medal awarded to four young mathematicians
description: Recognized as the Nobel Prize in mathematics, the Fields medal honors researchers under the age of 40 for outstanding discoveries. Of the four recipients, one winner, 35-year-old Hong Wang, is only the third woman to receive the honor since its inception in 1936.

headline: In Marseille, Chez Freddy's simple, sugar-coated chichis
description: 'Gourmet Kiosks' (2/6). Across France, small kiosks serving local specialties are a favorite stop for holidaymakers. Chez Freddy is a renowned stop for sweet fried dough.

headline: The mysterious horse-shaped geoglyph England has been maintaining for thousands of years
description: The Uffington White Horse is one of the best known geoglyphs in the UK. Drawn around 3,000 years ago on a hillside in Oxfordshire, its chalk outline is maintained each year by volunteers through a restoration campaign.

How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
description: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

headline: European Commission withdraws €2 million grant from Venice Biennale over Russian presence
description: The major contemporary art event had allowed Russia to return to the festival and present a pavilion there in May, before closing it to the public, sparking controversy and leading the festival's jury to resign.

headline: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'You can't fight French bureaucracy'
description: 'A Russian in Paris' (1/6). Journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya, who came to France in 2022 because of her opposition to the war in Ukraine, describes the difficulties she has faced with the prefecture while barely speaking the language.

headline: France's agriculture law puts government in a bind
'Stopping AI because it carries risks? That would be like banning cars because they cause accidents'
'Can we continue to entrust global sport to organizations more concerned with their financial interests than with the public good?'
